programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: L'industrie du potin artistique est-elle encore utile?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Geneviève Pettersen, Alex Perron, Catherine Beauchamp et Varda Étienne.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: La parité en politique passe-t-elle par une exclusion temporaire des candidats hommes?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Antonine Yaccarini, Me Alexis Wawanoloath, Rodolphe Husny et notre invitée, Maïka Sondarjée.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: La religion devrait-elle être exclue des funérailles et hommages offerts par l'État?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Déborah Cherenfant, Victor Henriquez, François Lambert et Alex Perron.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre les partys de bureau pour Noël?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Antonine Yaccarini, Victor Henriquez, François Lambert et Maude Goyer.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Les enseignants devraient-ils donner des devoirs aux élèves pendant la grève?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Geneviève Pettersen, Victor Henriquez, Varda Étienne et Maude Goyer.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Comité sur l'identité de genre : la présence d'une personne trans ou non binaire serait-elle essentielle?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Me Alexis Wawanoloath, Antonine Yaccarini, Victor Henriquez et notre invité le directeur général Rafaël Provost.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre laisser son ado boire de l'alcool?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Geneviève Pettersen, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, François Lambert et l'ancien directeur général d'Éduc'Alcool Hubert Sacy.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Devrait-on imposer des amendes aux commerces qui jettent leurs surplus alimentaires?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Victor Henriquez, Danny St Pierre et Déborah Cherenfant.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre une loi spéciale pour mettre fin à la grève?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Rodolphe Husny et l'enseignante et chroniqueuse en éducation Claudine Potvin.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Le Québec devrait-il créer un musée pour honorer la carrière de Céline Dion?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, Catherine Beauchamp, Varda Étienne et notre invité l'animateur et chroniqueur culturel Mike Gauthier.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre intervenir lorsqu'on est témoin d'un manque de civisme?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, Geneviève Pettersen et Antonine Yaccarini.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre offrir des cartes cadeaux aux fêtes?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, François Lambert, Geneviève Pettersen, Maude Goyer, Meeker Guerrier et Déborah Cherenfant.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre les résolutions de début d'année?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Maude Goyer et Victor Henriquez.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Rattrapage scolaire : devrait-on ajouter des jours de classe au calendrier?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Déborah Cherenfant et la chroniqueuse en éducation Claudine Potvin.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Est-ce que Denis Coderre pourrait être le « sauveur » du PLQ?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, Rodolphe Husny, Victor Henriquez et notre invité l'ancien ministre libéral Robert Poëti.

programme.title: The Patriot
programme.desc: A rural Montana community is threatened when the FBI tries to arrest members of a radical militia group. The militia retaliates by releasing a killer virus into the air, sickening most of the local residents -- but not Dr. Wesley McClaren (Steven Seagal) or his daughter, Holly (Gailard Sartain). It seems that Holly is immune to the virus. As McClaren races to find a cure, militia leader Floyd Chisolm (L.Q. Jones) attempts to kidnap Holly so that his group can make an antitoxin.

programme.title: Operator
programme.desc: Chaos ignites when the daughter of a veteran 911 operator and her estranged husband are held captive.

programme.title: American Yakuza 2: Back to Back

programme.title: Interview with the Repo Man

programme.title: Welcome Back Rotter
programme.desc: Matt and Froy crash the meeting of a fraternal lodge to ask for a car, but instead they get an angry old man with a rifle. Froy and Matt go on a repo that turns into a battle royale.

programme.title: Flame-Thrown for a Loop
programme.desc: Lou struggles to pick up the pieces after the accident leaves him needing Sonia more than ever. Lou and Carlos deal with a big man in a tiny car, but when Carlos lets his guard down he's delivered some serious blows.

programme.title: Bouquets and Blowtorches
programme.desc: The team returns from a successful mission in Vegas, but there's trouble brewing within the crew's ranks. Ronnie and Carlos repo from a self-defense instructor who lets his students do his dirty work, Matt and Froy confront a delinquent delivery man steeling flowers from graves, and Lou and Carlos are left defenseless against gang members with blowtorches. Can Lou still keep his cool in the high pressure repo business, or will there be repercussions for Matt and Froy that no one saw coming?

programme.title: Daddy's Little Girl
programme.desc: The destruction of the repo team's truck has left the future of the company in the balance, but Matt's got a trick up his sleeve that might keep them afloat. Lou and Sonia head to a dark alley to take a van away from a pair of libidinous teens with a dark secret, Sonia and Froy come face to face with the world's most ornery baker and come away a little worse for wear, and Matt and Froy get in way over their heads when they attempt a repo at a high-octane truck racing track.

programme.title: New Truck Day
programme.desc: The repo team's new truck finally arrives, and with it, a new chapter in the building feud between Team Lou and Team Matt.

programme.title: Cement Mixer
programme.desc: Down in Texas, Matt and Froy are staking out a cement mixer, but their repo turns disastrous when an innocent bystander gets showered in concrete. In LA, Frankie takes the reins with an alcoholic debtor, and Lou and Sonia deal with a diva who pushes Sonia to her breaking point.

programme.title: Probation Abomination
programme.desc: Sonia returns, but not before putting the team on probation and laying some ground rules.

programme.title: The Waiting Game
programme.desc: While Froy waits on news from his lawyer about his warrant in Texas, he considers taking matters into his own hands. Lou and Froy bust some slick moves in an attempt to wrangle a car from a debtor who is prepared to battle. Then, when one woman behaves like a child, Matt puts her in timeout, but worries he may have gone too far. And finally, Lou, Matt, and Froy take on an jealous husband with deadly aim.

programme.title: Gypsy Barbeque!
programme.desc: A jazz musician sets off Sonia and punches are thrown as Ronnie tries to manage the mayhem, and Matt and Sonia wrestle to bring deluded actors back to reality.

programme.title: Super-Sonia
programme.desc: Things get a lot more complicated for the team when Sonia snaps and puts herself on the business end of a knife and the gangbanger wielding it. Froy and Sonia wind up in the middle of a relationship dispute with an absent-minded professor who's got too many cars and too many girlfriends, Matt and Froy confront a debtor with a tiny motorcycle and an enormous mean streak, and Sonia and Lyndah find themselves in the middle of a gang dispute gone horribly awry.

programme.title: Taken for a Ride
programme.desc: A professional stunt driver slips through Froy and Sonia's grasp, sending them on a violent high speed chase. Matt offends a pretty RO who seeks womanly revenge, and Froy and Carlos go undercover at a lowrider show. And when Sonia announces a dangerous plan to save the company from the lawsuit, the team must decide whether they're willing to risk it all.

programme.title: Mile High Mayhem
programme.desc: On this special season finale of Operation Repo, the team gears up for a mile high repo of a lifetime! Froy and Sonia repo a water blob from a lake house, but the RO and his friends refuse to let this deflate their party. And with Sonia's hands tied, she is forced to offer Matt a position on the airplane. The team takes to the skies, but when plans go awry will Matt make the ultimate sacrifice for the team? Or will his ego force everything to spiral violently out of control?

programme.title: The Trouble with Tractors
programme.desc: The team's work with the anger management specialist starts to pay off with disastrous consequences. Sonia and Froy drive to the country to repo a tractor and are forced to confront a group of irate corporate hayriders, Lou and Sonia wrangle with a confused lady who is missing both her car and her sense of decency, and Matt and Sonia get into a Mexican standoff with a wealthy divorcee, his daughter, and her rageaholic boyfriend.

programme.title: Andrew Sleighter
programme.desc: Andrew Sleighter started in comedy looking for the perfect funny line to open a biography. Since then, Andrewís laid-back style and clever writing has won him appreciation from fans and respect from his peers. A regular at colleges and comedy clubs across the country, Andrewís also performed at some of the most prestigious comedy festivals in and out of the country including Bumbershoot, Bridgetown, The Cabo Comedy Festival, The Aspen Comedy Festival, Laughing Skull, and the Johnny Carson Great American Comedy Festival. Andrew wrote and blogged for the Sports Show with Norm Macdonald on Comedy Central and was a regular on the MTV prank show Money From Strangers. Heís performed on Nuvo TVís Stand Up & Deliver, Last Comic Standing, and made his late-night stand-up debut on Conan. Andrewís comedy has the ability to reach wide ranges of audiences and works in a diversity of rooms. Reliable and consistent, he rarely ends a show by lighting the stage on fire out of rage. Most importantly, heís constantly fascinated by and eager to keep learning about stand-up.

programme.title: Jordan Makin: Comedy in COVID
programme.desc: Jordan Makin delivers the vaccine to the people!

programme.title: Christian Pieper
programme.desc: Christian Pieper is an award-winning and crowd-pleasing comedian from Beaver Dam, Kentucky, now living in Los Angeles. In just five years as a performer, Christian has quickly earned acclaim for his honest, smart, and welcoming approach to personal material, layering unexpectedly subversive observations on current events, culture, and relationships.A two-time semifinalist in the prestigious Boston Comedy Festival, hes also been featured in the Crossroads Comedy Festival, Burbank Comedy Festival, and other festivals and competitions across the country. Christian has also performed abroad, having done shows for troops and civilians in eight different countries, and was a guest on the Bob & Tom show. He currently hosts and produces Adrenaline, a comedy-challenge show presented regularly at Wiseguys Comedy Club in Salt Lake City and the Hollywood Improv.

programme.title: Jeff Allen
programme.sub-title: I Can Laugh About it Now
programme.desc: Jeff Allen combines clean, hilarious humor like no other comedian working today. Besides performing at corporate functions and fundraisers, he regularly appears on television, radio, and casinos across the country. Consistently making heroes out of meeting planners, Jeff Allen provides world-class comedy.

See what creativity lies and thrives within, and meet those looking to unleash it. 1) How to design a library that makes kids want to read | Michael Bierut 2) Fake videos of real people -- and how to spot them | Supasorn Suwajanakorn 3) Can machines read your emotions? - Kostas Karpouzis 4) Inside Africa's thriving art scene | Touria El Glaoui 5) Why must artists be poor? | Hadi Eldebek 6) What is color? | Colm Kelleher 7) 3 creative ways to fix fashion's waste problem | Amit Kalra

programme.title: Larger-Than-Life Art
programme.desc: Artists who translate imagination and poignant worldviews into monumental, magnificent pieces of art. 1) Can art amend history? | Titus Kaphar 2) How we see color | Colm Kelleher 3) Why theater is essential to democracy | Oskar Eustis 4) What's the point(e) of ballet? | Ming Luke 5) Sculptures that'd be at home in the deep sea | Shih Chieh Huang 6) How volumetric video brings a new dimension to filmmaking | Diego Prilusky

programme.title: The Latest in Ideas
programme.desc: New, fresh ideas straight from the brightest minds of our time. 1) How to live a meaningful life | Brian S. Lowery 2) What does appendix pain feel like? | David R. Flum 3) What makes us human in the age of AI? A psychologist and a technologist answer | Brian S. Lowery and Kylan Gibbs 4) Why did it take so long to find giant squids? | Anna Rothschild

programme.title: Food for Thought
programme.desc: What can compare to the pleasures of the palate? Chefs, food scientists and passionate entrepreneurs celebrate all things edible -- and explore what's next for the future of food. 1) A cleaner world could start in a rice field | Jim Whitaker and Jessica Whitaker Allen 2) A brief history of dumplings | Miranda Brown 3) An interview with the Queen of Creole Cuisine | Leah Chase and Pat Mitchell 4) Is alternative meat the recipe for a healthier planet? | Tao Zhang

programme.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 4.13
programme.desc: Even if a snake is not poisonous, it should pretend to be venomous. Take a look at these limbless reptiles

programme.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 1.5
programme.desc: Get up close and personal with the creatures that call your backyard home.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Too Close for Comfort
programme.desc: Another chase through the hotbed of wicked weather puts the storm hunters a little too close to the danger zone. Will Mark & Jaclyn see the tornado they're hoping for before it's too late?

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Tornado Terror
programme.desc: Having witnessed the ravages of a deadly tornado just a few days earlier, on the last day of their chase the storm hunters are again shaken to the core by a giant, unpredictable tornado.

programme.title: Hellfire Heroes | Weather Extremes
programme.desc: A springtime blizzard has firefighters working overtime a pickup hits a semi a space heater sparks a fire.

programme.title: Hellfire Heroes | Wildfire Season
programme.desc: Firefighters burn grass fields hoping to prevent a repeat of the 2011 wildfires that devastated the town.

programme.title: Captured
programme.desc: A catastrophic earthquake, a mesmerizing avalanche, a destructive tornado, and an unintentional smashup derby on icy highways our experts weigh in on these viral videos as people get caught off guard by the sudden and powerful forces of nature.

programme.title: Captured
programme.desc: El Niño is a temperature anomaly in the Pacific Ocean that affects weather patterns around the globe. In this episode we show some of the costly and deadly weather that can result when a Super El Niño occurs.

programme.title: Captured
programme.desc: Tornadoes they are the embodiment of severe weather. In this episode we show some of the most terrifying weather caught on camera from surviving a direct hit to a tornado in a typhoon.

programme.title: Captured Extreme Weather | Vicious Winds
programme.desc: Volcanic ash engulfing a town, a massive firenado in California - just some of the viral weather videos caught on camera in this episode of Captured, as Vicious Winds take center stage.

programme.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 4.13

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: Blippi enjoys a day of playtime at Billy Bees in Anaheim, CA.

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: Blippi plays outside at the Children's Hands On Museum having fun making his own adventures.

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: Blippi has a big job to do! Can Blippi learn to use some big vehicles and make the job easy?

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: Grab your backpacks, and get ready to dig! Blippi spends the day as a paleontologist and learns to walk like a dinosaur with actual moving Dinosaurs!

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: Blippi heads to “CandyLand Indoor Playground” for a sweet and silly adventure!

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: Blippi has a big job to do! Can Blippi learn to use some big vehicles and make the job easy?

programme.title: Blippi

programme.title: Blippi

programme.desc: Blippi and Meekah celebrate Earth Day by learning to compost and by planting sage.

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: Blippi learns all about the amazing machines that can take you up in the sky.

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: Blippi is ready to take on the tiniest sport there is… mini golf!

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: Blippi and Meekah visit an emergency vehicle factory and see how a fire truck is built!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: My First Signs: See and Sign With Baby
programme.desc: As babies grow, their eye-hand coordination begins to improve as their ability to interact with their surroundings blossoms. It is the perfect time for My First Signs, A playful introduction to 20 common words and phrases from baby's world -- including ''mommy'', ''daddy'' and ''I love you'' -- both Spoken and in Sign Language.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: As little ones become better acquainted with the world, they grow curious about animals of all kinds, beyond just the family pet. And as their communication skills continue to develop, they enjoy imitating animal sounds as well. Baby Noah Animal Expedition takes your little explorer across the globe to meet animals -- two-by-two -- from the savannah, the rainforest and tropics, the Outback, the ocean and the Polar Regions.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Da Vinci: From Head to Toe
programme.desc: From the day their eyes open, babies begin to observe new things around the world and about themselves. Baby Da Vinci From Head to Toe presents them with a playful and interactive introduction to the human body in three languages -- English, Spanish, and French. With a whimsical blend of adorable puppets, real children and animals and famous works of art, this captivating program also features upbeat classical music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: World Music introduces you and your little one to beautiful sounds from every continent, bringing a fresh, new experience of music and culture. Sing, dance and play to exotic rhythms that open the door to an appreciation of different cultures from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Learn all about music and rhythm in the world around us! Discover classical music and learn instrument names through narration and repetition. Inspire sweet dreams with gentle melodies and a comforting bedtime story. Learn about classical music from Beethoven, Bach, and Mozart. Sing and dance along to classic rhythmic songs like The Wheels on the Bus and see real-life images of instruments.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: As babies continue to explore the world around them, they soon encounter many creatures, both large and small, that are extremely intriguing. Whether furry or feathered, winged or wiggly, bumpy or smooth, each holds a special fascination. Neighborhood Animals exposes little ones to animals most likely to be found close to home or in easily recognized environments.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby's First Moves: Get Up and Go
programme.desc: Within the first year of babies' lives, their physical development begins to kick into high gear as they explore ways to physically interact with the world around them. BABY'S FIRST MOVES is a joyful celebration of their movement milestones -- including first steps, clapping, jumping, dancing and more.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Baby Mozart is an introduction to Mozart's beautiful music, which has been proven to have positive affects on people of all ages, especially babies. Images of brightly colored toys and known objects aid in the audio presentation of child-friendly arrangements and sound effects.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Your little one will want to keep a beat, snap his fingers and march his feet! Join new characters Pillie, the musical woodpecker, and Coco, the dancing cockatoo, and share the excitement of rhythm with your baby. World of Rhythm explores rhythm in music, rhythm in nature and rhythm as a form of expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby's Favorite Places: First Words Around Town
programme.desc: As babies turn one year old, they continue to develop their communication skills. Meanwhile, with you as their guide, they also begin to explore the world beyond their front door. Baby's Favorite Places presents them with a playful introduction to 20 words for the names of familiar locations from the market, to the library, to the local park.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Every stage is an exciting adventure with your newborn. As babies grow, they absorb a wide variety of sights and sounds. And with so much to see and hear, infants seem to enjoy, and be most soothed by, gentle sounds such as your voice or pleasant, restful music. Treat your child -- and yourself -- to the delights of Baby Bach Musical Adventure, an award-winning program that exposes babies to the joy and majesty of classical music while presenting them with stimulating, colorful images.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Roley & The Rockstar / Scruffty The Detective
programme.desc: Bob is installing a garden pond for rock star Lennie Lazenby. / The museum curator is planning a Time Capsule.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Cock A Doodle Spud / Wendy's Surprise Party
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles needs Bob to build a hencoop. / Wendy is holding a surprise party for her sister Jenny.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building some traffic calming measures. Bob has been working all night laying lino.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Spud The Dragon / Pilchard Steals The Show
programme.desc: Bob is insulating Mrs. Potts' loft. Wendy is building an enclosure for the local dog show.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob's new metal detector is delivered. / Bob is installing a new pizza oven for Mr Sabatini.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Wendy's Removal Service / Mr Beasley's DIY Disaster
programme.desc: Bob is carrying out a loft conversion at a new home. / Bob is building a lean-to for Mr Beasley's allotment.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Bob's Egg And Spoon Race / Trix's Tiles
programme.desc: The whole town is practicing for an egg and spoon race. / Bob is tiling a roof.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Mr Sabatini's Smashing Day / Roley To The Rescue
programme.desc: Bob is rendering a wall for Mr Sabatini. / After a stormy night Wendy has to mend a chimney pot at Mrs Potts'.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Wendy's Magic Birthday / Bob's Egg And Spoon Race
programme.desc: It is Wendy's birthday, but she doesn't want any fuss. / The town is practicing for an egg and spoon race.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Mr Beasley's New Friends / Spud The Pilot
programme.desc: Bob is repairing a leaking water tank at school. / Bob is installing an antique fireplace for Mrs Percival.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Trix and the Otters / Speedy Skip
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles wants a fence built around his compost heap. / Bob is replacing a potting shed for Mr Beasley.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Bob and the Goalie / Dizzy Goes Camping
programme.desc: Bob is building a new exercise park. Farmer Pickles has decided to open a camp site on his land.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Mayor asks Bob to build a Pets Corner in a local park. / A burst pipe outside Mr Sabatini's needs fixing.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Lofty's Long Load / Hamish's New Home
programme.desc: Bob is making a through-lounge at the Bentleys'. / Mr Fothergill has a nasty case of dry rot.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Bob The Photographer / Mr Bentley's Trains
programme.desc: Bob goes to Farmer Pickles farm to take some photographs. / Mrs Bentley asks Bob to build a new model railway.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry is made to feel self-conscious about an old, patched-together rain coat that she has. When the kids put on a play version of Cinderella, Strawberry and the others are able to see that it's not what you wear that counts, but what you are.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Peppermint Fizz wants to exclude a new girl, Blueberry Muffin, because she's "different" - in the way she looks, lives, talks, etc.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry's friend Coco Calypso lives on Seaberry Beach with her pet, Papaya Parrot, and a band of fun-loving monkeys. When the seaberries that Coco harvests from the sea are stolen, she asks Strawberry, Rainbow Sherbet and Blueberry Muffin to solve the mystery.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake's friendship letter to children around the world elicits an invitation from Crepes Suzette to visit Pearis and the Pieffel Tower. Some of the other children and Custard are not so sure that it is wise to visit a strange land.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Friends from all over the world descend on Strawberryland to take part in the first Great Friendship Festival. Strawberry thinks the festival will provide a wonderful opportunity to see the world through different eyes.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: When the Berry Fairy Came to Stay
programme.desc: Out gathering berries one day, Strawberry & her pets find a tiny fairy among the berry vines. Realizing that Strawberry is a very kind & caring person, the little fairy decides to take advantage of the kid's good nature.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: One windy day, a tiny fairy named Sherry Bobbleberry gets blown into Strawberry's open window. She is a klutzy little thing who is convinced she will always be a major goof-up.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: At the urging of Honey Pie Pony, Strawberry & Friends decide to put on a Cooking Show. Ginger Snap invents a version of TV, which she calls Berryvision, to broadcast the show all over Strawberryland.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's that time of year when the berries are about to burst into bloom - and it looks like this is going to be a bumper crop! Strawberry decides to celebrate the event with a first-ever Berry Big Berry Blossom Festival.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: The Berry Blossom Festival is underway and everyone in Strawberryland is having a Grand Time, thanks to the selfless efforts of Strawberry Shortcake: she's really giving this festival all she has!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's winter and the pond's frozen and that means Strawberry and friends can ice skate! They decide to showcase their talents at an Ice Dance Show. But just then, a new kid named Apricot shows up.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Strawberry & her friends decide to put on a talent show, they plan to enter themselves as a band, The Strawberry Jams!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry and her friends are getting the band back together to perform at a local festival, but Ginger Snap is out of town.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake has always dreamed of seeing a certain rare flower, which only blooms once every ten years - and then only for a day!

programme.title: Pain and Gain
programme.desc: Go Onizuka, a popular duelist on Link VRAINS, accepts an offer from SOL to duel Playmaker. He wants to prove himself and he sees Playmaker as a powerful opponent. He lures Yusaku into a trap and a Speed Duel begins.

programme.title: Blippi's School Supply Scavenger Hunt
programme.desc: Blippi embarks on a fantastic back-to-school adventure!

programme.title: The Big Freeze
programme.desc: It's another windy winter day, and the postal bees are staying indoors—the wind is too strong for their berry bitty wings. “We don't know when the next mail will get through!” It's bad news for Blueberry, who has been anxiously awaiting the next installment of a Patty Persimmon Detective story. If only she could get to the post office in Berry Bitty Dale where her package is waiting! She gets a brainstorm. Cutting up the side of an old broken tennis racket, she attaches the pieces to her rain boots—instant ice skates! Fashionably bundled against the cold, and pulled by butterflies who sail on the wind like kites, she sail-skates down the frozen road, to the amazement of her friends.

programme.title: On Ice
programme.desc: It appears winter is finally coming to an end and Raspberry is planning a big fashion show to display her spring line. Her vision is to have the girls model her fashions in a parade past the town fountain. What she didn't realize is that the Berrykins are turning the fountain into a big water show. Every time the girls come to rehearse, they must comically dodge jets of fruit juice from the out-of-control fountain—the Berrykins are still working out the bugs. Strawberry reminds them that this is a public area that should be shared, and suggests they work out a schedule.

programme.title: Blippi's Adventure and Discovery Tour
programme.desc: Join Blippi as he embarks on an exciting Adventure and Discovery Tour! Explore the fascinating jobs at Wimbledon, enjoy a fun-filled game of Mini Golf, and cheer on your favorite team at Liverpool FC. Watch Blippi get hands-on with recycling in Smash My Trash, and meet Stanley the Dinosaur for a roaring good time. Each adventure is packed with fun, learning, and new discoveries, making every moment with Blippi an unforgettable experience for kids.

programme.title: Blippi's Learning and Exploration Journey
programme.desc: Set off on a Learning and Exploration Journey with Blippi as he dives into the world of construction vehicles, exploring bulldozers and excavators up close! Discover the wonders of a Children's Museum and learn through play at the Cayton Children's Museum. Get a splash of color and knowledge as Blippi teaches kids about plants and colors in a fun and engaging way. These educational adventures are perfect for curious young minds, making learning exciting and interactive with Blippi.

programme.title: On the Road
programme.desc: Strawberry, Cherry and the girls form a new band, Strawberry and the Sweet Beats, and prepare to go on the road to perform at the Opening Ceremony of the Berry Big City Games! While master mechanic Berrykin Bruce turns Cherry's tour bus into the band's hotel-on-wheels, Strawberry and her friends pack for the big trip. But the rest of Berry Bitty City points out that someone needs to run the girls' businesses while they are gone. Strawberry and her friends give the Berrykins a crash course in city-sitting.

programme.title: A Boy and His Dogs
programme.desc: Berrykins Ed and Earl report to Princess Berrykin and Strawberry that they are certain there is a monster in the berry patch! They saw something with many noses and many tails making weird noises in the bushes. But upon further investigation by Strawberry and her friends, it is discovered that friendly boy Huckleberry Pie was driving his pet-mobile of orphan dogs through the countryside, took a wrong turn and got stuck near Berry Bitty City. What the Berrykins mistook for a monster was really the bunch of cute pups Huck has been trying to get adopted. While Huck's van is being repaired, Strawberry invites Huck and his seven pups to spend the night in the new bedroom above the marketplace, while she bunks in her old bedroom above the café. The next morning, Strawberry discovers that the playful pups have given her bedroom a total makeover –the wrong kind. An embarrassed Huck insists on staying until he can repair the damage.

programme.title: Must see TV!
programme.desc: There's just one problem with his plan: sitting around and not moving would make his body feel terrible. At school Sid and his friends learn that exercising is a really fun way to stay healthy! And it makes your body feel great!

programme.title: Sid's Health Day
programme.desc: At school, Sid and his friends discover that there's lots of ways to stay healthy: washing hands to avoid germs, eating nutritious foods, brushing teeth and exercising!

programme.title: Camp New-Act One
programme.desc: Penny, Nattie, Emmie, and Jess are best friends who are spending their summer working at a creative arts center called All Things New. Together, they stage Art in the Park, a festival created for children to experience the joy of the arts. However, when a top talent scout shows up and offers Emmie's adopted brother, Isiah, a life of fame and fortune in Los Angeles, Isiah is forced to make some difficult choice that may compromise his beliefs, as well as his relationships with his family and the kids who look up to him at the center. Filled with music, dancing, art, comedy, and drama, New is a fun family film that inspires viewers of all ages to use their gifts for the Lord.

programme.title: The Maze Of Amazement
programme.desc: The Knights find themselves trapped in a maze looking for the Book of Deception. But will they actually get their hands on it, or will it keep deceiving them? The Knights travel to the Hill Country in the northern part of Knighton to Axl's home town of DIGGINGTON (where they realize that all of Axl's family look just like him.) While they're there, Merlok 2.0 detects something…one of the Magic Books! It's “The Book of Deception.” They go to find it and discover it ended up under a mountain. And, to make matters worse, the magic book has built a maze around itself to keep it from being found (it is deceptive!) As the NEXO-Knights enter the maze from one side, Jestro enters from the other the Book of Monsters has led him to the maze to get The Book of Deception, too. It's a race through this confusing maze to see who can get the book first. Ava figures out the maze is the Whackman maze from a videogame. After help from Axl's people to defeat Jestro and win the book, the knights discover it's a FAKE BOOK of deception. The Book of Deception created a fake copy of itself!

Then, while practicing, she has an accident and hurts her ankle. She insists it's no big deal, but once she heals and tries to resume practice, the usually fearless Mia finds she's now terrified of snowboarding! With the help of her Friends, she tries hard to overcome her fear, but her nightmarish visions of snowboard monsters and jeering snowmen have her shaking in her bed. programme.title: Charles Bradley, William Aukstik, Frederick Deboe & Michael Deller
programme.sub-title: Charles Bradley: The Screaming Eagle Of Soul
programme.desc: This concert of soul singer Charles Bradley (1948-2017) was recorded in an intimate setting in Paris in September 2014. Before considering a career in music, Bradley had long tried to make ends meet with a series of small jobs. It was only in 1996 that he finally followed in the footsteps of his idol and soul icon James Brown, with whom he shared an exceptional ability to bring audiences to ecstasy. Bradley has developed a unique sound that combines funk, soul, and R&B, but still tinged with Brown influences.

programme.title: Errol Garner
programme.sub-title: Erroll Garner - Live in Singer Concertzaal, Laren
programme.desc: On June 18, 1962, American pianist and composer Erroll Garner performed at the Singer Concertzaal in Laren. The vibrant and joyous concert was played with drummer Kelly Martin and bassist Eddie Calhoun, each long-standing collaborators from Garner's trio. The Singer Concertzaal in Laren, the Netherlands is a concert hall and museum dedicated to the preservation of the art collection of American William Henry Singer. It features a variety of artists, both visual and aural.

programme.title: Christian McBride
programme.desc: On July 20, 2018, double bassist Christian McBride presented his band ‘New Jawn' at the Malta Jazz Festival. A five-time Grammy winner, McBride is one of the most requested, most recorded, and most respected figures in the music world today. Hailing from Philadelphia, this music luminary combines jazz, R&B, pop/rock, hip hop/neo-soul, and classical. Gracing the Malta Jazz stage with him are Nasheet Waits (drums), Marcus Strickland (tenor sax), and Josh Evans (trumpet).

programme.title: John Patitucci, Marco Pignataro, Joe Lovano & Terri Lyne Carrington
programme.sub-title: The Tällberg Foundation: "Jazz for the Planet
programme.desc: On October 19, 2021, John Patitucci (bass), Marco Pignataro (sax and musical director), and Joe Lovano (sax) showcased inspirational new originals at GBH's Fraser Performance Studio in Boston, MA, accompanied by Terri Lyne Carrington (drums), Nadia Washington (vocals), Chico Pinheiro (guitar), and Anastassiya Petrova (piano). This recording, titled 'Tällberg's Jazz for the Planet', was made in support of the Tällberg Foundation's message and aims to inspire positive climate action. Since 1981, the Tällberg Foundation has been trying to nurture new thinking, in part by looking at societal challenges through the lenses of artists.

programme.title: Rahsaan Roland Kirk
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Michael Mossman
programme.sub-title: Munich Philharmonic Hall: Michel Camilo Quintet
programme.desc: Well-known for his exuberant style and intimate familiarity with a vast range of jazz and Latin idioms, Michel Camilo has always had a sixth sense for musical adventure. Camilo was one of the most stimulating jazz pianists to emerge in the mid- to late '80s. His powerful two-handed attack (full of impressive technical skills), his knowledge of both jazz and Afro-Cuban music, and his willingness to constantly take chances turn each of his solos into an adventure. This broadcast was taped during the 1991 Münchner Klaviersommer, when Camilo was playing with his amazing Quintet. He is accompanied by Ned Mann (bass), Cliff Almond (drums), Michel Mossman (trumpet and trombone) and Ralph Bowen (saxophone and flute). Camilo's rise to fame has been swift, and his influences range from jazz musicians like Art Tatum and Keith Jarrett to twentieth-century composers like Debussy and Ravel.

programme.title: Anita Oday
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: China Moses, Joe Armon Jones & Luke Wynter
programme.sub-title: China Moses: 'Whatever' at Jazz à Ramatuelle
programme.desc: In 1988, Dee Dee Bridgewater was one of the first great jazz singers to perform at the Jazz Festival in Ramatuelle. In 2016, the story seems to repeat itself as her daughter China Moses took the stage at Ramatuelle. Over the years, China Moses has become a mature performer with a unique style: rocky voice, sharp sense of swing, and intense/energetic stage presence. After signing compilations dedicated to Dinah Washington and blues, Moses released in 2016 an album of his own compositions, entitled "Whatever". Get ready for a most entertaining performance combining jazz, soul, and hip-hop.

programme.title: Quatuor Ebène
programme.desc: Renowned French string quartet Quatuor Ébène marked the 250th birth anniversary of Ludwig van Beethoven (1770-1827) with a remarkable project: recording all of the great composer's sixteen string quartets. For five years, violinists Pierre Colombet and Gabriel Le Magadure, violist Marie Chilemme, and cellist Raphaël Merlin immersed themselves in Beethoven's 650 pages of sheet music. Their efforts culminated in the performance of the composer's complete repertoire for string quartet, which covers three decades of Beethoven's musical creativity, during six impressive concerts at Philharmonie de Paris in the autumn of 2020. Quatuor Ébène explored every facet of Beethoven's string quartet repertoire: from the youthful Opus 18 string quartets to the Razumovsky, Harp, and Serioso quartets (Opus 59, 74, and 95) from his middle period, and finally, the depth of his late quartets (Opus 127 to 135). This program features Quatuor Ébène performing Beethoven's String Quartet No. 3 in D major, Op. 18 No. 3 String Quartet No. 11 in F minor, Op. 95, “Quartetto Serioso” and String Quartet No. 8 in E minor, Op. 59, No. 2, "Razumovsky". This concert performance was recorded at Philharmonie de Paris on November 23, 2020.

programme.title: Nikolay Lugansky
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Martha Argerich, Evgeny Kissin, Theodosia Ntokou & Cristina Marton-Argerich
programme.sub-title: Martha Argerich – A Birthday Celebration - II
programme.desc: On the occasion of her 80th birthday, Argentinian pianist Martha Argerich explored repertoire for piano and violin in this wonderful concert, recorded at Château de Chantilly, France. The ‘Grande Dame' of the piano is joined by various renowned artists, including pianists Evgeny Kissin, Theodosia Ntokou, and Cristina Marton-Argerich, and violinist Maxim Vengerov. On the program are Dmitri Shostakovich's Concertino for two pianos, Op. 94 Frédéric Chopin's Impromptu No. 3, Op. 51, Polonaise No. 6, Op. 53 “Heroic”, and Waltz No. 6, Op. 64 No. 1 Camille Saint-Saëns's Polonaise for two pianos, Op. 77 César Franck's Sonata for violin and piano in A major and Fritz Kreisler's Liebesleid and Schön Rosmarin. This performance was recorded on June 12, 2021.

programme.title: Elena Bashkirova, Juliane Banse, Emmanuel Pahud & Claudio Bohorquez
programme.desc: The extraordinarily talented pianist Elena Bashkirova has been president of the Felix Mendelssohn-Bartholdy Foundation in Leipzig since October 2020, succeeding legendary conductor Kurt Masur. In this concert at the Mendelssohn House in Leipzig, Bashkirova is joined by soprano Juliane Banse, flutist Emmanuel Pahud, cellist Claudio Bohórquez, clarinettist Karl-Heinz Steffens, and pianist Ohad Ben-Ari in a chamber music program dedicated to the music of Mendelssohn and his sister Fanny Hensel. The program opens with Mendelssohn's Piano Trio in D minor, Op. 49. This is followed by a selection of songs: Mendelssohn's Schilflied ‘Auf dem Teich, dem regungslosen', Op. 71 No. 4, and Reiselied ‘Der Herbstwind rüttelt die Bäume', Op. 34 No. 6 Hensel's Vorwurf, Op. 10 No. 2 Verlust, Op. 9 No. 10 and Sehnsucht, Op. 9 No. 7 Mendelssohn's ‘Allnächtlich im Traume seh ich dich', Op. 86 No. 4 ‘Die Liebende schreibt', Op. 86 No. 3 and Nachtlied ‘Vergangen ist der lichte Tag', Op. 71 No. 6. The program continues with Mendelssohn's Seven Songs without Words, for piano four-hands (Op. 62 Nos. 1-6, and Op. 67 No. 1), and comes to a close with Mendelssohn's Three pieces for clarinet, violoncello and piano (arr. Ernst Naumann), comprising Prelude, Op. 35 No. 4, Songs without Words, Op. 53 No. 2, and Duet, Op. 38 No. 6. This performance was recorded in November 2020.

programme.title: Ivan Fischer & Budapest Festival Orchestra
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Herbert Blomstedt & Lucerne Festival Orchestra
programme.sub-title: Beethoven - Symphony No. 3 in E-flat major, Op. 55
programme.desc: Maestro Herbert Blomstedt makes his debut with the Lucerne Festival Orchestra at 2020's Lucerne Festival conducting Ludwig van Beethoven's celebrated Symphony No. 3 in E-flat major, Op. 55, “Eroica”. The sprightly Swedish conductor celebrated his 93rd birthday just one month before this performance. This remarkable performance was recorded at the Lucerne Culture and Congress Center (KKL), Switzerland, in August 2020.

programme.title: Nirvana
programme.desc: Very few bands have had a musical and social impact on the world however, in its short but monumental career Nirvana certainly did both. This film features powerful footage of the band in action, both in concert and in the studio. In addition, we a team of leading music critics and insiders including Miti Adhikari, the producer of 1991's historic BBC sessions, and Kurt Cobain's biographer Charles R. Cross. Features highlights from: Smells Like Teen Spirit, Heart Shaped Box, Rape Me, Sliver, Negative Creep, Pennyroyal Tea and more...

programme.title: Various Artists
programme.desc: The best of Qello Concerts

programme.title: Primal Scream
programme.desc: Primal Scream is a Scottish rock band originally formed in 1982 in Glasgow by Bobby Gillespie (vocals) and Jim Beattie. The lineup consists of Gillespie, Andrew Innes (guitar), Martin Duffy (keyboards), Simone Butler (bass) and Darrin Mooney (drums). Barrie Cadogan has toured and recorded with the band since 2006, as a replacement after the departure of guitarist Robert "Throb" Young.

programme.title: Paul Weller
programme.desc: Starting out with the band The Jam (1976–82), Weller branched out to a more soulful style with The Style Council (1983–89), before establishing himself as a successful solo artist in 1991. He is also the principal figure of the 1970s and 1980s mod revival, and is often referred to as The Modfather.

programme.title: Various Artists
programme.desc: The best of Qello Concerts

programme.title: Daryl Hall & John Oates
programme.sub-title: Live at The Sydney Entertainment Centre
programme.desc: When Daryl Hall and John Oates took to the stage at Sydney's Entertainment Centre as the iconic bass line of ‘Maneater' began to play, the fans knew they were in for a treat. This duo may have been opening concerts with that irresistibly smooth number for years, but it never seems to lose its magic, and the crowd – an eclectic mix of teens and baby boomers alike – lapped it up. Daryl Hall and John Oates wisely reunited a few years ago after a string of solo projects, and although Oates no longer sports his signature ‘tache, they put on the kind of show that makes it hard to believe they even considered going their separate ways. Earworms like ‘I Can't Go For That', ‘Out Of Touch' and ‘Kiss On My List' were surefire reminders that these guys are absolute hit machines, and their catchy synth-soul classics probably deserve to permeate the airwaves as much today as they did decades ago. These Philly crooners still have it in bucket-loads, so this show should have everyone from diehard Daryl Hall and John Oates fans to kids of the 80's groovin' along in no time.

programme.title: Lana Del Rey
programme.desc: Born to Die is the second album by singer-songwriter Lana Del Rey, and the first to be officially released on January 27, 2012 and that would lead to global success In countries such as the United Kingdom the album was the best selling of that year, that recognition made her a guest of honor in one of the most popular London festivals iTunes Festival, where she would open the awe of all attendees on the night of September 25 2012 at the Roundhouse in the city of London.

programme.title: Flying Over Spanish Islands
programme.desc: In their first collaboration, cinematographer Stefan Zimmerman and Nature Relaxation™ producer David Huting present "Above the Spanish Islands." This 50-minute 4K aerial film captures Mallorca and the Canary Islands, set to ambient music by Diamans, Electus, and Michael St. Laurent.

programme.title: Flying Over Fiji Islands 2
programme.desc: "Flying over Fiji Islands 2" offers a 1-hour and 42-minute journey across Fiji's majestic landscapes. Combining nature sounds with ambient music by The Cynic Project, it features smooth ground-level and aerial shots, concluding with a breathtaking real-time sunset over the sea.

programme.title: Flying Over Bermuda
programme.desc: "Flying Over Bermuda" offers an 80-minute relaxation journey above Bermuda's turquoise waters and coastlines in 4K Ultra HD. Filmed by Noel Thomas, it features sights like Horseshoe Bay and the Vixen shipwreck, with music by The Cynic Project and other artists

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland: Part I" provides a soothing aerial journey through eastern Iceland's fjords and valleys, starting at sunrise in Brei_dalsvik. Enjoy views of mists, a waterfall, and the Brei_dals' river, set to nature sounds and music by The Cynic Project, showcasing East Iceland's beauty.

programme.title: Flow of Life
programme.desc: Flow of life is a relaxing film full of beautiful images of amazing landscapes. Mountains, Forests, Creeks and Rivers as well as Butterflies, Bees, Bumblebees, lizards and more. Also included are gentle breath reminders to assist in a meditative experience. All scored with relaxing music, composed by Chris Collins.

programme.title: Flying Over The Atlantic Ocean
programme.desc: Travel the majestic coasts of the Atlantic Ocean from a bird's-eye view.

programme.title: Sunny Getaways
programme.desc: Spend a restful moment in Maui, a tropical paradise of gleaming white sand beaches and turquoise waters.

programme.title: Return to Paradise 2
programme.desc: Escape to one of the world's most idyllic tropical islands for 2 hours of relaxing on the beach with "Return to Paradise,." The sequel to "A Day in Paradise" (2014), the film returns viewers to the hidden exotic Fijian island of Nanuku - a tiny palm-covered sandy atoll surrounded by magical lagoons brimming with sea life. The film lasts for 2 full hours and features a dynamic mix of stunning tropical island beach & palm tree scenes that will gently transport viewers around the island. Each scene lasts several minutes, allowing time to soak up the views & absorb the healing energies which paradise provides! There are also some aerial drone clips to give the viewer perspective from above.

programme.title: Turtle Paradise 2
programme.desc: Leave your worries from the world above behind and dive into a vibrant undersea world inhabited by the iconic sea turtles, for 2 full hours of Nature Relaxation. Captured by filmmaker collaborators Tilo & Yvonne Kühnast, Turtle Paradise features hundreds of unique real-time video scenes captured from around the Caribbean, paired with gentle underwater sounds & calming music for deep relaxation by Jason Stephenson.
